Producing one ton of export-grade coconut shisha charcoal costs about $1,455 (Rp 24,734/kg) at Rp 13,500/kg raw material and Rp 17,000/USD. At a 25% margin the factory sells at about $1,819/t and keeps roughly $364/t before tax.

Producing one ton of export-grade coconut shisha charcoal costs about $1,455 (Rp 24,734/kg) at Rp 13,500/kg raw material (verified 15 July 2026; factory model).

At a 25% margin the factory selling price is about $1,819/t, of which roughly $364/t is factory EBITDA (verified 15 July 2026).

Raw material is about 68% of total factory cost once the 25% production material loss is counted (verified 15 July 2026).

What one ton of shisha charcoal costs to produce (raw material Rp 13,500/kg, Rp 17,000/USD) · verified 15 July 2026
Cost lineRp / kg finished≈ USD / t
Raw material — carbonized coconut shell (Rp 13,500/kg + 25% production loss)Rp 16,875$993
Unloading from truck (+ 25% loss)Rp 63$4
Tapioca binderRp 616$36
Oven drying (wood-pellet)Rp 200$12
Production wagesRp 1,850$109
Container stuffingRp 50$3
ElectricityRp 500$29
MaintenanceRp 350$21
Office & miscRp 750$44
Production subtotalRp 21,254$1,250
Inner plastic bagRp 300$18
Labour — inner plasticRp 300$18
Inner boxRp 1,825$107
Labour — inner boxRp 150$9
Master boxRp 650$38
Labour — master boxRp 100$6
StickerRp 105$6
Silica gelRp 50$3
Packaging subtotalRp 3,480$205
Total factory costRp 24,734$1,455
Factory margin (25% on cost)—$364
Factory selling price—$1,819

Per-kg costs of finished charcoal. Raw material and truck unloading carry the 25% material lost in cleaning, grinding and drying — you buy more shell than you sell as briquette.

Selling price by raw-material price and margin

Factory selling price (USD/t) by raw-material price and margin · verified 15 July 2026
Raw material10% margin15% margin20% margin25% margin
Rp 12,000/kg$1,479$1,546$1,614$1,681
Rp 13,500/kg$1,600$1,673$1,746$1,819
Rp 15,000/kg$1,722$1,800$1,878$1,957
Rp 17,000/kg$1,883$1,969$2,055$2,140

At Rp 17,000/USD, packaging included. Margin applied as a markup on cost.

What the factory actually keeps

What the factory keeps per ton (USD, EBITDA before tax) · verified 15 July 2026
Raw material10% margin15% margin20% margin25% margin
Rp 12,000/kg$134$202$269$336
Rp 13,500/kg$145$218$291$364
Rp 15,000/kg$157$235$313$391
Rp 17,000/kg$171$257$342$428

EBITDA, not net income — 5–10% of production typically fails and must be remanufactured.

How this is calculated

Every input is bought in Indonesian rupiah, so the model works per kg of finished charcoal in IDR and converts to USD per ton at the end. Raw material and truck unloading are multiplied by 1.25 because 25% of the material is lost to rotary cleaning, grinding, extrusion waste and drying. Fixed lines — tapioca binder, oven fuel, wages, container stuffing, electricity, maintenance and office — are added per kg, then retail packaging (inner plastic, printed inner boxes, master carton, stickers, silica gel and their labour).

The margin is a markup on cost: selling price = cost × (1 + margin). What the factory keeps is EBITDA before tax; 5–10% of production typically fails QC and is remanufactured, and equipment financing sits outside this model. Cleaner grades lose more material — see the price-to-grade check for how loss changes the standard price per grade.

Frequently asked questions

How much does it cost to produce shisha charcoal?

About $1,455 per ton (Rp 24,734/kg) all-in at the factory — raw material at Rp 13,500/kg including the 25% production loss, plus binder, fuel, wages, electricity and retail packaging.

How much profit does a shisha charcoal factory make per ton?

At a 25% margin on cost, about $364 per ton EBITDA before tax. Across common margins the range is roughly $145–$364/t — and 5–10% of production fails and is remade.

Why does the raw-material price matter so much?

Carbonized coconut shell is about 68% of total factory cost, and every kilogram of finished charcoal needs 1.25 kg of it because of the 25% production loss. A Rp 1,500/kg move in raw material shifts the selling price by roughly $138/t.

Run the factory model yourself

Change the raw-material price, exchange rate and margin to see cost, price and profit.

Last verified 15 July 2026. Figures are illustrative industry ranges, not quotes — verify before relying on them. Freight and the US–Indonesia tariff change most often.
